# Introduction, part 1: Understanding the basics 

## R programming and RStudio

### Installing R and RStudio
R is a free software environment for statistical computing and graphics. Find out more at the [R project site](https://www.r-project.org/).

RStudio is an integrated development environment (IDE*) for R. Find out more at the [RStudio site](https://www.rstudio.com/products/rstudio/).

#### Instructions

1. Download [Rv4](https://cran.r-project.org/mirrors.html) (as of August 2021)
    a. Select mirror* site that is closest to your geographic location.
    b. Select appropriate link for OS (Linux, MacOS, Windows).
    c. Download `base`, run and follow the instructions.
    d. *If on windows*, download `Rtools`, run and follow the instructions.
2. Download [RStudio Desktop](https://www.rstudio.com/products/rstudio/download/).  

Yay! You're ready to start programming!

3. Open RStudio and install the [`tidyverse`](https://www.tidyverse.org/) package.
4. Load the `tidyverse` library.

> **HINT**: You will need the functions `install.packages()` and `library()`. If you type `?function_name()` into the *console panel* of RStudio and hit enter, you will be able to see the docuumentation of the function in the *help panel*. This documentation will tell you the inputs and outputs for that function. At the bottom of the *help panel* will be a few examples of the function's usage. More information on R documentation can be found [TODO].

![](figures/rstudio_gui.png)


## Math concepts for bioinformatics

## NCBI resources: PubMed and GEO
